route.name: Three-day Samuel P. Taylor loop
route.sourceNote: Current local GPX/GeoJSON route with verified gravel and Point Reyes Seashore variants from prior planning work.
route.description: Three-day Samuel P. Taylor loop - bike-in first night order

days.name: Day 1 - Fairfax to Samuel P. Taylor
days.camp: Night 1: Samuel P. Taylor hike-and-bike / bike-in camp

days.name: Day 2 - Samuel P. Taylor to Olema via Point Reyes / Inverness
days.camp: Night 2: Olema Campground tent site - online availability seen for Jun 28-29

variants.name: Cross Marin / Inkwells
variants.sourceNote: Extracted from the Fairfax to Samuel P. Taylor GPX route segment between Samuel P. Taylor camp and Inkwells, then mirrored as an out-and-back.

variants.name: Bolinas Ridge sampler
variants.sourceNote: Copied from the previously generated Samuel P. Taylor alternates GPX/GeoJSON route: SPT to Bolinas Ridge Out-and-Back.

variants.name: Bolinas Ridge short
variants.sourceNote: First 5 miles of the routed Bolinas Ridge sampler from Samuel P. Taylor, mirrored as an out-and-back.

variants.name: Nicasio detour
variants.sourceNote: Optional paved/mixed-road return that adds Nicasio scenery and miles. Use only if the group wants a longer Day 3.

variants.name: Kent Lake detour
variants.sourceNote: Optional Fairfax-to-SPT approach through the Lagunitas/Kent Lake watershed area. This is a much bigger first day.

variants.name: Day 2 Seashore variant
variants.sourceNote: NPS Bicycle Riding page and Bear Valley Trail route page; OSM NPS-imported trail geometry.

variants.name: Day 2 south loop
variants.sourceNote: NPS Bicycle Riding page, Bear Valley Trail route page, Wildcat/Stewart bike route page, Five Brooks Trailhead page.

logistics: Samuel P. Taylor official page last checked July 2, 2026 7:46 AM shows reservation availability NO for Fri July 10 and Sat July 11, YES for Sun July 12 and Mon July 13. https://www.parks.ca.gov/?page_id=469

Samuel P. Taylor lists hike-and-bike campsites, restrooms/showers, drinking water, and bike trails; campsite availability for the target Saturday remains unresolved. https://www.parks.ca.gov/?page_id=469
Subagent identified Samuel P. Taylor as the most plausible local overnighter direction, but only with explicit hiker-bike risk handling.
Backup lodging or transit bailout is required if the hiker-bike site is full.
Plan Ready pilot source audit checked July 5, 2026 for Samuel P. Taylor Loop.
Bailout source: Marin Transit Route 68 serves San Rafael, Fairfax, Lagunitas, Samuel P. Taylor State Park, Olema, Point Reyes Station, and Inverness.
Parking source: Samuel P. Taylor State Park official page lists vehicle day-use fees, Camp Taylor entrance, Devil's Gulch pull-out parking, and park address.

review.decision: promote to plan_ready source coverage only; gate status remains blocked until date-specific gates pass
blockers: Samuel P. Taylor official availability shows NO for Friday July 10 and Saturday July 11 as of July 2, 2026.
This route depends on Samuel P. Taylor as a core overnight anchor unless a different confirmed itinerary is selected.
Samuel P. Taylor-centered plan cannot be gate-confirmed until the exact GPX/exportable line and camp anchor are both confirmed for the intended dates.
Samuel P. Taylor reservable availability is not a confirmed July 10-11 anchor; hiker-bike is first-come for arriving cyclists and has group-size limits.
First-come hiker-bike capacity means a same-day backup plan is required.
Core Samuel P. Taylor camp anchor is not reservable for July 10-11.
Hike-and-bike or Pantoll/Bootjack first-come camping cannot be treated as gate-confirmed without a confirmed backup.
Bike legality is only partial until the exact shortened line is audited against Samuel P. Taylor and Mount Tam bike-legal corridors.
Core Friday/Saturday camp anchor is not publicly available in the official reservation summary.
Hike-and-bike same-day capacity is listed as a facility but not publicly date-confirmable online.
No public GPX found for the exact local Samuel P. Taylor variant.
Core Samuel P. Taylor overnight is unavailable for July 10 and July 11 in the official reservation table.
Hike-bike capacity is first-come and cannot be used as exact public availability.
Exact bike-legal line audit remains incomplete.
requiredFixes: Confirm a legal July 11 hiker-biker/walk-up spot by phone or choose a different camp/lodging sequence.
Refresh fire/red-flag, water, and route access checks after the camp anchor is solved.
Verify exact GPX/exportable line for the shortened Samuel P. Taylor-centered route.
Confirm hiker-bike rules, group limit, arrival procedure, and backup lodging/transit.
Recheck fire restrictions, AQI/smoke, and Marin forecast within 72 hours.
Replace first-come camping with reserved camping or confirmed lodging, or add a guaranteed same-day backup.
Audit the exact GPX against Samuel P. Taylor bike rules and avoid non-bike-legal singletrack.
Refresh CAL FIRE, NWS, AirNow, and park closure pages within 72 hours of departure.
Shift to the publicly available July 12/13 window, confirm hiker-bike by phone, or choose a different confirmed camp/lodging anchor.
Replace this with a public-GPX route or publish/verify the exact GPX source before reconsidering gate confirmation.
Find a publicly confirmed July 10/11 overnight anchor or shift to dates with confirmed availability.
Audit the exact line against official Samuel P. Taylor bicycle rules and maps.
For Gate Confirmed, choose and verify the exact overnight anchor; public availability previously showed core dates blocked.
For Gate Confirmed, audit every GPX segment against Samuel P. Taylor and adjacent land-manager bike rules.
For Gate Confirmed, refresh Samuel P. Taylor trail status, NWS, AirNow/CAL FIRE, and any adjacent park alerts close to departure.
openQuestions: What changed in the July 9-10 final refresh for closures, weather, smoke, water, and camps?

promotionChecklist.targetWindowNote: Source coverage is now sufficient for Plan Ready, but campsite availability and date-specific conditions remain operational gates.
promotionChecklist.officialGeometryNote: Workspace contains an exportable local GPX, but no public source GPX equivalent was found for this exact variant.
promotionChecklist.campingPermitsNote: Samuel P. Taylor reservation table shows Fri July 10 and Sat July 11, 2026 as NO; hike-bike capacity is first-come and cannot be used as exact public availability.
promotionChecklist.closuresFireWeatherNote: Samuel P. Taylor page lists seasonal creek closure, North Creek washout, Bill's Trail seasonal closure mechanics, and current trail-contact source; NWS/AirNow still require date-specific refresh.
promotionChecklist.legalAccessNote: Samuel P. Taylor lists bike trails and the brochure distinguishes multi-use routes, but the exact local loop still needs line-by-line legality audit.
promotionChecklist.waterResupplyNote: Park facilities list restrooms/showers and drinking water, but the unavailable overnight anchor prevents a complete water/camp plan.

verification.notes: Local GPX/GeoJSON and PDF artifacts are usable, but the July 10-13 camp/resource gate is not verified.

tips: Do not present this as next-weekend gate-confirmed until the July 11 overnight anchor is confirmed.
Use the existing GPX/PDF as planning artifacts only.
If Samuel stays unavailable, revise the itinerary around confirmed lodging/camp before promotion.

readinessEvidence.sourceName: California State Parks Samuel P. Taylor page
readinessEvidence.finding: Reservation availability shows NO for Friday July 10 and Saturday July 11; the current route cannot be gate-confirmed for the target weekend without a confirmed alternate overnight anchor.
Local route geometry, variants, elevation, and PDF artifacts are usable for planning, but not enough for gate confirmation while camping is blocked.
Subagent found Samuel P. Taylor hiker-bike mechanics plausible but not confirmed enough for gate-confirmed.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: California State Parks Samuel P. Taylor pages via subagent triage

readinessEvidence.finding: Reservation availability shows NO for Fri 7/10/26 and Sat 7/11/26; page lists hike-and-bike campsites, drinking water, and bike rules limiting bikes to Cross Marin Trail, fire roads, and seasonal Bill's Trail.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: California State Parks Samuel P. Taylor

readinessEvidence.finding: Exportable local GPX exists with 3 tracks and 2396 trackpoints, but exact shortened gate-confirmed line and camp anchor remain unresolved.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: Local route-library Samuel P. Taylor GPX

readinessEvidence.finding: Samuel P. Taylor State Park's official page showed reservation availability NO for July 10 and July 11, 2026, but YES for July 12 and July 13, 2026 as two-consecutive-night starts.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: California State Parks Samuel P. Taylor

readinessEvidence.finding: Workspace contains an exportable local GPX, but no public source GPX equivalent was found for this exact variant; this fails the public-GPX-first gate.

Samuel P. Taylor reservation availability table says two-night starts Fri 7/10/26 NO and Sat 7/11/26 NO; Sun 7/12/26 and Mon 7/13/26 YES. Hike & Bike is first-come, first-served, max 8 persons, not exact date-confirmable online.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: California State Parks Samuel P. Taylor

readinessEvidence.finding: Local GPX/route geometry exists in the app for the Samuel P. Taylor Loop and is the current geometry source until replaced with an external public GPX.
Samuel P. Taylor State Park states bicycles are allowed only on Cross Marin Trail, fire roads, and seasonal Bill's Trail, and not on single-track walking trails.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: California State Parks Samuel P. Taylor

readinessEvidence.finding: Samuel P. Taylor State Park page lists current restrictions, seasonal creek closure, North Creek trail washout, Bill's Trail seasonal closure mechanics, and park phone for most current trail information.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: California State Parks Samuel P. Taylor

readinessEvidence.finding: NWS San Francisco Bay Area office is the weather/fire-weather source for Marin departure-window checks.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: National Weather Service San Francisco Bay Area

readinessEvidence.finding: AirNow is the smoke/AQI source for San Rafael/Marin-area departure-window checks.

Samuel P. Taylor State Park page links ReserveCalifornia and shows reservation availability plus hike-and-bike/tent/group/remote camping facilities; exact trip date remains blocked until an overnight is confirmed.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: California State Parks Samuel P. Taylor / ReserveCalifornia

readinessEvidence.finding: Samuel P. Taylor State Park lists drinking water available and states all park water is potable, while also noting RV dump-station/water-tank fill service reductions; this is the water source for the park anchor.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: California State Parks Samuel P. Taylor

readinessEvidence.finding: Marin Transit Route 68 serves San Rafael, Fairfax, Lagunitas, Samuel P. Taylor State Park, Olema, Point Reyes Station, and Inverness and has bike racks; this is the bailout/transit source.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: Marin Transit Route 68 / West Marin Stagecoach

readinessEvidence.finding: Samuel P. Taylor official page lists vehicle day-use fees, Camp Taylor entrance, Devil's Gulch pull-out parking, and park address; this is the parking/trailhead source.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: California State Parks Samuel P. Taylor parking and directions

readinessEvidence.finding: Local static build/export pipeline verifies map artifacts; exact navigational export QA remains local-artifact based.

route.surfaceMix: Mixed pavement, fire road, rail-trail, and optional gravel add-ons; use variants for more dirt.
route.bestSeason: Spring through fall; winter can work if storms and mud-sensitive trails are avoided.

difficultyProfile.traffic: review paved connectors and Highway sections
difficultyProfile.technical: low to moderate unless variants add singletrack/fire-road roughness

resources.water: Water/resupply appears available from prior planning, but check current campground and town status.
resources.food: Confirm stores/restaurants and hours near daily endpoints.
resources.camping: Night 1: Samuel P. Taylor hike-and-bike / bike-in camp; Night 2: Olema Campground tent site - online availability seen for Jun 28-29; Finish: Fairfax
resources.toiletsShowers: Confirm per campground; hiker-biker sites vary.
resources.bikeShops: Identify nearest bike shops before gate confirmation.
resources.longestGap: Not fully automated; reviewer should verify longest water/resupply gap.
resources.mosquitoes: Lower than Sierra lake routes, but still check season and camp reports.

mapQuality.reviewRule: Do not promote beyond idea if terrain/topographic context is missing or route lines are hard to read.

promotionActions.action: Resolve target weekend fit before gate confirmation.
